title: fix(docs): Fix glossary FIXME tags
body:
<!--
Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs
Fixes #31628 

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.


#### Any other comments?
While working on [#31628](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/31628), I encountered a `ModuleNotFoundError` due to a misnamed Sphinx extension (`sphinx-prompt` instead of `sphinx_prompt`). This PR also fixes that to ensure local doc builds (`make`) work correctly.

#### Setup
```
Python 3.13.3

   machine: macOS-15.5-arm64-arm-64bit-Mach-O

Python dependencies:
      sklearn: 1.8.dev0
          pip: 25.0.1
   setuptools: None
        numpy: 2.3.1
        scipy: 1.16.0
       Cython: 3.1.2
       pandas: 2.3.0
   matplotlib: 3.10.3
       joblib: 1.5.1
threadpoolctl: 3.6.0

Built with OpenMP: False
```

step_plan.summary: Fix the documentation build by correcting the Sphinx extension import name and remove user-facing `FIXME` placeholders from the glossary so published docs are clean and local `make -C doc html` succeeds.

steps.operation: Fix the Sphinx extension module name in the docs build configuration
steps.reason: Local doc builds fail with `ModuleNotFoundError` because Sphinx tries to import the extension by the string listed in `extensions`; the importable module name is `sphinx_prompt` (underscore), not `sphinx-prompt` (hyphen). Updating the extensions list (at module scope) ensures `make -C doc html` can import the extension successfully.
steps.side_effects: Docs builds will now require that the `sphinx-prompt` *distribution* (PyPI package) provides the `sphinx_prompt` module as expected; if CI/build images pin different doc requirements, they may need to be aligned. No effect on scikit-learn runtime code, only documentation build configuration.
